summary: Matched the ftCo_Down common fighter module by moving a block of down-state routines out of asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s into src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Down.c and adding a dedicated ftCo_Down.h. The PR reports 100% matching for the new file, covering ftCo_Down_CheckInput, ftCo_80098324, ftCo_Down_Anim, ftCo_Down_IASA, ftCo_Down_Phys, ftCo_Down_Coll, and ftCo_80098400. It also updated callers in ftCo_DownBound.c to use the named ftCo_Down_CheckInput instead of ftCo_80098214, added the new object to obj_files.mk, and refined ftCommonData field types x248/x24C from unknowns to float.
what_changed: Deleted the ftCo_80098214 through ftCo_80098400 block and associated sdata2 constants .L_804D8620, .L_804D8624, and .L_804D8628 from asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Down.c with matched implementations for down input checking, down motion-state transition helper, animation/IASA/physics/collision callbacks, and ftCo_80098400.
Added src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Down.h with prototypes for ftCo_Down_CheckInput, ftCo_80098324, ftCo_Down_Anim, ftCo_Down_IASA, ftCo_Down_Phys, and ftCo_Down_Coll.
Added ftCo_Down.c.o to TEXT_O_FILES in obj_files.mk before ftdynamics and before the remaining ft_08A4 asm object.
Updated ftCo_DownBound.c to include ftCo_Down.h and replace ftCo_80098214 calls with ftCo_Down_CheckInput in ftCo_DownBound_Anim and ftCo_DownWait_IASA.

Removed ftCo_80098214 and ftCo_80098324 declarations from ft_08A4.h, moved/typed ftCo_8009856C as a static-style helper declaration taking ftCo_GObj* and FtMotionId, and typed ftCo_800DF644/ftCo_800DF678 as bool-returning helpers.
Changed ftCommonData fields x248 and x24C in src/melee/ft/types.h from UNK_T to float, matching their use as thresholds in the new ftCo_Down.c logic.
Added an ft/forward.h include to ftCo_DownStand.h, apparently to satisfy forward declarations introduced by the header reshuffle.
smart_moves: Created a domain-specific ftCo_Down.c/ftCo_Down.h pair instead of leaving matched routines in the broad ft_08A4 bucket, improving future searchability and modularity around common down-state behavior.
Gave ftCo_80098214 the descriptive name ftCo_Down_CheckInput at the call sites, making down-bound/down-wait control flow more readable without over-renaming every still-unknown helper.
Kept difficult functions guarded with inline assembly under matching macros while also providing C fallback/representative logic, preserving exact matching while documenting intended behavior.

Factored recognizable condition checks into small static inline helpers inlineA0 and inlineB0 in the C path, exposing that x248/x20 are stick-angle/input thresholds and x24C is used against fp->x67C/x67D.

Reused existing motion-state helpers rather than duplicating side effects: ftCo_80098324 calls Fighter_ChangeMotionState, ftAnim_8006EBA4, and ftCommon_8007CCE8; ftCo_80098400 delegates to ftCo_8009856C.
Converted relevant ftCommonData fields to float at the same time the C logic proved they are compared via floating-point loads, avoiding placeholder drift.
decomp_lessons: When extracting a contiguous asm range from a shared asm file, remove the exact functions and their now-owned local constants from the asm file and add the new C object in obj_files.mk near the old asm object to preserve link order.
Even if a routine still needs inline asm for MUST_MATCH, placing it in the correct source module with a C alternative can document semantics and unlock typed headers/call-site cleanup.
Use real field types when instruction evidence is clear: lfs/fcmpo uses around p_ftCommonData offsets x248 and x24C justified changing those fields to float.
Call-site renames are valuable matching cleanup: replacing ftCo_80098214 with ftCo_Down_CheckInput in existing state code made the decompiled control flow self-explanatory.

Small callback wrappers often match naturally as direct calls: ftCo_Down_Anim checks ftAnim_IsFramesRemaining before ft_8008A2BC, ftCo_Down_Phys calls ft_80084FA8, and ftCo_Down_Coll calls ft_80084104.
Motion-state selection can often be recovered from numeric IDs by comparing current motion state and input/facing direction, then mapping to existing FtMotionId enum names.
naming_conventions: Matched common fighter down-state files use the ftCo_Down prefix and live under src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/.
The previously address-named ftCo_80098214 was renamed to the behavior name ftCo_Down_CheckInput.
Standard fighter callback suffixes appear as _Anim, _IASA, _Phys, and _Coll.
Still-unknown helpers retained address-style names such as ftCo_80098324, ftCo_80098400, and ftCo_8009856C rather than forcing speculative names.
Header guard for the new file follows the long path-based style MELEE_FT_CHARA_FTCOMMON_FTCO_DOWN_H.
The PR uses existing enum spellings such as ftCo_MS_DownFowardU and ftCo_MS_DownFowardD as-is, including the apparent existing 'Foward' spelling.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)` around an inline asm implementation of ftCo_Down_CheckInput, with a readable C implementation in the else branch.
Used `#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO` around inline asm for ftCo_80098324, with a C fallback that calls Fighter_ChangeMotionState(gobj, msid, Ft_MF_None, NULL, 0, 1, 0), ftAnim_8006EBA4, and ftCommon_8007CCE8.
Inline asm labels were converted from `.L_80098248` style to `lbl_80098248` style inside the C asm block.
The inline asm references literal floats such as 0.0f and 1.0f instead of retaining the removed sdata2 labels from ft_08A4.s.
The C path expresses ABS(fp->input.lstick.x) >= p_ftCommonData->x248 and ftCo_GetLStickAngle(fp) < p_ftCommonData->x20 as the left-stick down input check.
The C path expresses the ftCo_80098400 gate as fp->x67C < p_ftCommonData->x24C || fp->x67D < p_ftCommonData->x24C || ftCo_800DF644(fp).
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace inline asm in ftCo_Down_CheckInput with fully matching C.
Find later PRs that rename ftCo_80098324, ftCo_80098400, or ftCo_8009856C.
Search for additional ftCommonData x248 and x24C references to assign semantic field names.
Inspect nearby ftCo_DownBound and ftCo_DownStand PRs for shared down-state naming and header patterns.
Search for other PRs using MUST_MATCH inline asm plus C fallback as an intermediate matching tactic.
